setup: RALPH_PROJECT_DIR=/project, RALPH_WORKER_COUNT=3, tasks.json has 5 tasks with dependencies
assertions: Reads $RALPH_PROJECT_DIR and $RALPH_WORKER_COUNT from env
Reads .ralph/tasks.json to build dependency graph
Identifies claimable tasks (pending with deps met)

setup: tasks.json has setup task (id:1, no depends_on) and 3 parallel tasks depending on it
assertions: Assigns setup task to worker-1 only
Does NOT activate workers 2 and 3
Updates tasks.json: task 1 status to in-progress

setup: Worker pane 0 has title ralph-worker-1, running zsh
assertions: Uses tmux send-keys to launch claude on the pane
Passes RALPH_WORKER_ID, RALPH_TASK_ID, RALPH_PROJECT_DIR as env vars
Includes --dangerously-skip-permissions and /ralph-kage-bunshin-loop

setup: Task 2 in-progress, worker 1 on pane 0
assertions: Reads worker-1 state.json dod_checklist
Spawns architect review on the same pane (pane 0)
Does NOT mark task as converged yet

setup: Task 2 in-progress on worker 1, tasks 3,4 depend on task 2
assertions: Sets task 2 status to converged in tasks.json
Re-evaluates dependency graph for newly claimable tasks
Assigns next claimable tasks to available worker panes

setup: Task 2 in-progress on worker 1, pane 0
assertions: Writes rejection reasons to worker-1 state.json architect_review
Task stays in-progress in tasks.json
Spawns new worker Claude session on the same pane to retry

setup: Task 2 in-progress on worker 1, pane 0
assertions: Spawns debugger on the same pane
Uses /ralph-kage-bunshin-debug skill
Passes RALPH_WORKER_ID and RALPH_TASK_ID env vars

setup: Debugger just ran for task 2, worker 1
assertions: Writes diagnosis to worker-1 state.json debug_session
Resets consecutive_failures to 0
Spawns new worker Claude session to apply the fix

setup: Task 2 in-progress on worker 1, tasks 3,4 are also pending
assertions: Resets task 2 to pending in tasks.json
Sets pathology.oscillation to true in worker-1 state.json
Considers assigning a different task to the worker pane

setup: Task 1 was setup (converged now), tasks 2,3,4 depend on 1, RALPH_WORKER_COUNT=3
assertions: Activates 3 worker panes simultaneously
Assigns tasks 2,3,4 to workers 1,2,3 respectively
Each gets a fresh Claude session

setup: Task 3 has lease_expires_at in the past, worker pane shows zsh (not claude)
assertions: Detects expired lease in tasks.json
Resets task to pending
Re-assigns the task to an idle pane

setup: Worker 2 state.json updated_at is 15 minutes ago, pane still running claude
assertions: Detects stale updated_at (>10 min)
Checks pane state before resetting
Takes appropriate action (reset or wait)

setup: Tasks 1-4 already converged, task 5 was the last
assertions: Detects all tasks are converged
Sends macOS notification
Prints summary with task names and worker assignments

prompt: Need to send command to worker 2
setup: Pane indices may have shifted after kills
assertions: Resolves pane index by title (ralph-worker-2) not by index
Uses tmux list-panes with pane_title filter
Never assumes pane N = worker N

setup: Normal operation
assertions: Only the watcher writes to tasks.json
Worker does not modify tasks.json
Architect does not modify tasks.json

setup: Worker 1 just had its task approved, new claimable task available
assertions: Spawns a new Claude session on the pane
Does NOT reuse the architect session
New session starts with clean context

prompt: [BROADCAST] worker-2: API endpoint /v2/items returns 404, use /v3/items instead
setup: Worker 2 discovered API change
assertions: Logs the broadcast
Notes the discovery for future task assignments
Does NOT crash or ignore the message

prompt: Only 1 claimable task with 3 worker panes
setup: RALPH_WORKER_COUNT=3, only task 2 is claimable
assertions: Activates only worker-1 for the task
Workers 2 and 3 remain as empty shells
No tokens wasted on idle workers

setup: Task 3 in-progress, lease about to expire
assertions: Renews task lease_expires_at to now + 30 minutes
Task stays in-progress
Worker gets a fresh session to retry

prompt: Worker is stuck, fix the code
setup: Worker reports failure
assertions: Does NOT write any source code
Spawns a debugger to diagnose
Orchestrates, does not implement
